"venue": "Dental Materials", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Biochemical aging of a commercial methacrylate adhesive (Adper Scotchbond Multi-Purpose) in simulated human salivary esterase led to a 20% decrease in interfacial fracture toughness (KIC) after 6 months.", "direct_quote": "biochemical aging of the C-MA in SHSE led to a significant decrease (p < 0.05) in KIC (20 %) and fracture mode of either the experimental or commercial adhesive.", "direct_quote": "thermal aging had no effect (p > 0.05) on the interfacial mechanics and fracture mode", "fact_category": Relevant as general adhesion aging science but specific to dental, not DVS-PEI encapsulants."}, {"source_url": "https://doi.org/10.15517/ijds.2020.39198", "source_title": "In Vitro Effect of Temperature on Dentin Bond Strength of Universal Adhesive Systems", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.5, "overall_relevance": 0.2, "year": 2020, "authors": [], "venue": "International Journal of Dental Sciences", "doi": "10.15517/ijds.2020.39198", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "The lowest shear bond strength (SBS) values were observed in samples at 4 degrees C while the highest were at 55 degrees C, with statistically significant difference (p<0.05).", "direct_quote": "the lowest SBS values were observed in the samples at 4 C while the highest SBS values were observed in the samples at 55 C", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.22, "confidence": 0.85, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["shear bond strength", "4 C", "55 C", "temperature"]}, {"statement": "Adhesive effectiveness may increase if preheated at 36 degrees C or above before use rather than applied at refrigerator or room temperature.", "direct_quote": "the effectiveness of an adhesive may increase if it is preheated at 36 C or above before use", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.18, "confidence": 0.8, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["adhesive preheating", "36 C", "bond strength", "temperature"]}], "evidence_summary": "Study on temperature effects on dental adhesive bond strength. Shows higher application temperature improves bond strength. Off-topic for DVS-PEI encapsulant damp heat aging research."}]}
